status_notes: Initial research from Google Cloud API Gateway and Cloud Endpoints documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live GCP environment).
notes: Managed API gateway for serverless backends. Also covers Cloud Endpoints for GKE/Compute. Security includes API keys, authentication, service control.

match_nodes.description: API keys with application and API restrictions

data.description: API keys identify the calling application (not the user). Keys can be restricted by application type (HTTP referrer, IP address, Android/iOS app) and by API (which APIs the key can call). API keys are for identification and quota management, not authentication.

options.description: Restrict which applications can use the key
Restrict which APIs the key can call
Per-key quota limits via API config or Service Control

data.vendor_recommendation: Always apply both application restrictions and API restrictions to API keys. API keys identify the calling project for quota and billing — use authentication (JWT) for user identity.

audit.console: Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials

data.description: Validate JSON Web Tokens (JWT) from identity providers. Configured in the OpenAPI spec via the securityDefinitions and x-google-audiences fields. Supports Firebase Auth, Google ID tokens, Auth0, Okta, and any custom OIDC provider.

options.description: Validate Google-signed OIDC tokens (service accounts, Cloud Identity)
Validate Auth0 JWT tokens via OIDC discovery
Validate Okta JWT tokens via OIDC discovery
Any provider with a JWKS endpoint

data.vendor_recommendation: Always require authentication for production APIs. Configure authentication requirements in the OpenAPI spec securityDefinitions section. Use x-google-audiences to validate the intended audience.

audit.note: Review the OpenAPI spec attached to the API config for securityDefinitions and security requirements.
audit.console: Google Cloud Console → API Gateway → {API} → API configs

configure.openapi_spec:
securityDefinitions:
  firebase:
    authorizationUrl: ""
    flow: implicit
    type: oauth2
    x-google-issuer: "https://securetoken.google.com/{project}"
    x-google-jwks_uri: "https://www.googleapis.com/service_accounts/v1/metadata/x509/securetoken@system.gserviceaccount.com"
    x-google-audiences: "{project}"
configure.note: Authentication is defined in the OpenAPI spec, not via CLI flags.

match_nodes.description: Quota management, billing, and logging per API consumer

data.description: Google Service Control manages quota, billing, and logging for API consumers. Tracks usage per API key and enforces quota limits defined in the API config. Provides per-consumer usage metrics and logging.

options.description: Define rate limits and daily quotas in the API config
Track API usage for billing purposes per consumer project
Log API requests with consumer identity and status

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure quota limits in the API config to prevent abuse. Monitor usage via Cloud Monitoring dashboards. Use consumer project-based quotas for multi-tenant APIs.

audit.console: Google Cloud Console → API Gateway → {API} → Metrics

configure.note: Quota limits are defined in the OpenAPI spec via x-google-management section.
configure.openapi_spec:
x-google-management:
  metrics:
    - name: read-requests
      valueType: INT64
      metricKind: DELTA
  quota:
    limits:
      - name: read-limit
        metric: read-requests
        unit: 1/min/{project}
        values:
          STANDARD: 100

match_nodes.description: IAM controls for API and gateway management

data.description: IAM roles control who can manage API Gateway resources (APIs, configs, gateways). Separate from consumer authentication — IAM governs the management plane, not the data plane (API calls).

options.description: Full control over API Gateway resources
Read-only access to API Gateway resources

data.vendor_recommendation: Follow least-privilege for management access. Separate API management roles from API consumer access. Use service accounts with minimal permissions for CI/CD pipelines deploying API configs.

audit.console: Google Cloud Console → IAM & Admin → IAM

match_nodes.description: Extensible Service Proxy v2 security features

data.description: Extensible Service Proxy v2 (ESPv2) is the runtime proxy for Cloud Endpoints deployed on GKE, Compute Engine, or Cloud Run. Provides TLS termination, CORS handling, JWT validation, and service control integration. ESPv2 is the underlying proxy for both API Gateway and Cloud Endpoints.

options.description: Terminate TLS at the proxy with custom certificates
Built-in health check endpoint for load balancer integration

data.vendor_recommendation: For Cloud Endpoints on GKE/Compute Engine, deploy ESPv2 as a sidecar container. Ensure TLS is configured for the proxy. For API Gateway (fully managed), ESPv2 is managed by Google — no direct configuration needed.

audit.note: For Cloud Endpoints: check ESPv2 container configuration and startup flags. For API Gateway: proxy configuration is managed automatically.

configure.note: ESPv2 is configured via startup flags (--ssl_server_cert_path, --cors_preset, etc.) when deployed as a container for Cloud Endpoints.
